content_markdown: Two identical containers P and Q hold two different ideal gases at the same temperature. The number of moles of each gas is the same. The molecular weight of the gas in container P is twice that of the gas in Q. The ratio of the pressure in P to that of Q will be
vibe_explanation: According to the ideal gas law $PV = nRT$, the pressure $P$ depends only on the volume $V$, the number of moles $n$, and the temperature $T$. Since the containers are identical ($V_P = V_Q$), the temperatures are the same ($T_P = T_Q$), and the number of moles are the same ($n_P = n_Q$), the pressure must be the same regardless of the molecular weight of the gas particles. Therefore, the ratio is 1.
